relations: Climate change is causing habitat loss, changes in food sources, and more frequent extreme weather events, all of which impact animals
The loss of sea ice in the Arctic due to climate change is directly affecting polar bears by reducing their primary hunting grounds
Warming temperatures due to climate change are melting sea ice in the Arctic
Warmer ocean temperatures caused by climate change are leading to coral bleaching, affecting coral reefs and the species that rely on them
Climate change has contributed to severe wildfires in Australia, affecting wildlife
Climate change, through events like wildfires, has pushed koala populations closer to the brink of extinction
Climate change has led to warmer winters, allowing the spread of white-nose syndrome among bat populations
Polar bears are facing habitat loss in the Arctic due to melting sea ice caused by climate change
The melting of sea ice in the Arctic, due to climate change, is reducing the primary hunting grounds for polar bears
Warmer ocean temperatures are causing coral bleaching, which affects the health of coral reefs and the marine life that depends on them
Wildfires in Australia have decimated koala populations
White-nose syndrome, exacerbated by climate change, affects bat populations
